-----

## name: Submit an Idea to the Lab
about: Have a radical question or conjecture? Run it through the Charter. Let’s find out what it’s made of.
title: “[IDEA] “
labels: needs-structuring
assignees: justindbilyeu

## Your Question or Idea

*One to three sentences. Be specific. Vague questions produce vague science.*

<!-- Write your idea here -->

-----

## Why You Think It Might Be Real

*One piece of evidence, observation, or intuition that made this question stick in your head. Not proof — just what pointed you toward it.*

<!-- What made you think this? -->

-----

## What Would Change Your Mind

*What would falsify it? What result would make you say “I was wrong”? If you can’t answer this, the idea isn’t ready yet — and that’s okay, keep thinking.*

<!-- What would prove you wrong? -->

-----

## Context (Optional)

*Relevant background, related work, or prior art you’re aware of. Skip if you don’t have any.*

<!-- Anything else we should know? -->

-----

**What happens next:**

The lab reviews submissions weekly. If your idea compiles — meaning it has a claim, a falsification path, and something to test — it gets run through the Research Assistant Charter. The adversarial review team (Claude, Sage, Gemini, DeepSeek, Grok, Kimi) stress-tests it and reports back in this thread.

If it doesn’t compile yet, you’ll get a note on what’s missing.

If it’s out of scope, it gets archived with an explanation.

No credentials required. No institutional affiliation required. Just an honest question and a willingness to be wrong.

*The credential is not the point. The rigor is the point.*
